title: Peloton Interactive Inc (PTON) Q3 2024 Earnings Call Transcript Highlights
text: Peloton Q3 2024 Earnings Highlights. Leadership Transition: Barry McCarthy stepped down as President and CEO during this period, with interim co-CEOs managing the company while the board searches for a permanent leader. Q3 Financial Metrics: Total revenue of $718 million within guidance, with subscription revenue of $438 million representing 61% of total revenue. Connected fitness revenue of $280 million representing the hardware segment. Subscriber Updates: The company ended Q3 with 3.06 million paid connected fitness subscriptions, reflecting a net increase of 52,000 in the quarter. Average net monthly paid connected fitness subscription churn was 1.2%, which outperformed internal expectations. App Performance: The company ended the quarter with 674,000 paid app subscriptions, reflecting a net reduction of 44,000 in the quarter. Restructuring Announcement: Peloton announced a new restructuring program to reduce annual expenses by more than $200 million. This included a workforce reduction of approximately 15% or about 400 global team members. Financial Position: The company ended Q3 with $795 million in cash on hand, providing financial flexibility for the turnaround.
